title: vicia vulgaris
intro: Vicia vulgaris, also known as common vetch or garden vetch, is a perennial legume native to Europe, western Asia, and northwest Africa. This plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Vicia vulgaris include its ability to aid in respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its potential to lower blood sugar levels and reduce inflammation. Vicia vulgaris can be prepared into various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
